proposal_text: This research proposes to apply sentiment analysis and linguistic style detection to a corpus of 5,000 anonymised undergraduate essays submitted to the School of English at Trinity College Dublin. The aim is to investigate whether emotional tone and writing style correlate with academic grade outcomes. Essays will be collected from the past three academic years with student consent obtained retrospectively via email. The NLP pipeline will extract features including sentiment polarity, subjectivity scores, vocabulary complexity, and sentence structure patterns. The researchers will investigate whether detectable stylistic differences correlate with students' first language, gender, or socioeconomic background as indicated by fee status. All analysis will be performed on pseudonymised data with student IDs replaced by random codes. Results will be published in aggregate form only.

assessment.risk_summary: The research proposal involves processing of personal data, including undergraduate essays with student consent obtained retrospectively. The analysis will investigate correlations between emotional tone, writing style, and academic grade outcomes, as well as detectable stylistic differences based on students' first language, gender, or socioeconomic background.

identified_risks.explanation: The research involves processing of personal data, including undergraduate essays with student consent obtained retrospectively.

The analysis will investigate correlations between emotional tone, writing style, and academic grade outcomes, as well as detectable stylistic differences based on students' first language, gender, or socioeconomic background.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Respect privacy — obtain informed consent where required and protect personally identifiable information (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Obtain explicit consent from students for processing their personal data.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Consider and ameliorate biases in data and biased code/model outputs (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Investigate potential biases in the analysis and take steps to mitigate them.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Assess and communicate degree to which datasets are representative of intended population — claims of diverse representation must be substantiated (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Assess the representativeness of the dataset and provide evidence to support any claims of diversity.

historical_precedents.incident_title: Detroit Police Facial Recognition False Arrest
historical_precedents.lesson: Carefully evaluate the accuracy and fairness of any facial recognition technology used in research.
Regularly audit and test algorithms for bias, especially when making decisions about individuals.

recommended_mitigations.mitigation: Use pseudonymised data to protect student identities.
Implement measures to detect and prevent biases in the analysis.

tier1_mandatory_actions: Obtain explicit consent from students for processing their personal data.
Investigate potential biases in the analysis and take steps to mitigate them.
tier2_reflective_prompts: How will you ensure that your research is representative of diverse populations?
What measures will you take to prevent biases in your analysis?
